reasoning: The question asks if the number of protests in a specific 30-day period (ending 2026-01-24) will exceed the 30-day average computed over a previous 360-day reference period (which currently sits at 3.167). Protest event data is naturally right-skewed, meaning that protests are highly episodic and clustered in time. A few months with many protests pull the annual average up, which causes the majority of individual months to fall below this mean. Thus, the median number of protests in any random 30-day period is generally lower than the overall mean. With a mean of ~3.17, at least 4 protests would be needed to exceed it. Absent specific information that January 2026 will coincide with a major localized spike (like the recent cost-of-living protests), mean reversion and base distribution shape suggest a probability below 50%, typically around 30-40%.
